title: DAC 2026 Mach42:  A new generation of physics-driven AI models for analog circuit verification - Semiwiki
gist: Oxford-physics spinout Mach42, which pivoted from fusion-energy plasma simulation in 2021, sells physics-driven AI surrogate models (trained via smart sampling against Cadence Spectre testbenches) that accelerate analog circuit verification by orders of magnitude and can auto-generate Verilog-A behavioral models that previously took weeks or months to build by hand.

themes.theme: Physics-embedded AI vs. traditional neural nets for circuit verification

insights.claim: Mach42's models embed the governing physical equations of a system directly into the network rather than merely capturing statistical input-output relationships, representing a departure from prior generations of neural networks and enabling the model to emulate underlying physics with much higher accuracy for analog circuit verification.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Positions physics-informed AI (versus pure statistical ML) as the differentiator for analog verification acceleration — a technical thesis relevant to evaluating competing AI-for-EDA approaches in the analog/mixed-signal space, which has historically resisted automation more than digital design.
insights.quote: The approach relies on embedding the governing equations of the system in the network, which now has a chance of emulating the underlying physics, rather than simply capturing statistical relationships between inputs and outputs.

insights.claim: Mach42's surrogate models, once trained, can be instanced directly inside the target simulation environment (normally Cadence Spectre) to provide orders-of-magnitude simulation speedup versus traditional non-linear equation solvers, because the model executes a trained neural network rather than solving equations from scratch.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: An 'orders of magnitude' speedup claim for analog verification (historically a slow, compute-bound bottleneck in chip design) is a significant potential productivity lever for analog/mixed-signal design teams if it holds up in production use.
insights.quote: the model can be instanced in the target simulation environment providing orders-of-magnitude simulation speedup (remember, we are executing a neural network instance, not a non-linear equation solver)

insights.claim: Mach42 can generate surrogate models that produce behavioral descriptions of circuits in the Verilog-A language automatically, a process the company says can otherwise take many weeks or months to complete manually.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Automated Verilog-A generation directly attacks a known manual bottleneck in mixed-signal SoC verification (behavioral modeling for system-level simulation), a concrete productivity claim to validate against customer case studies.
insights.quote: This enables designers to rapidly create highly accurate Verilog-A representations of their circuits automatically – a process that can take many weeks or months manually.

insights.claim: Mach42 originated as a 2019 University of Oxford Department of Physics spinout building tools for accelerating plasma/fusion-energy simulations, before pivoting after 2021 seed funding to target analog circuit verification as a fundamental simulation bottleneck in the semiconductor market.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: The fusion-energy-to-semiconductor pivot signals Mach42's core IP (physics-simulation acceleration) is domain-agnostic, suggesting potential for cross-application of its technology beyond EDA if the semiconductor bet doesn't fully pan out.
insights.quote: Mach42 spun out of the Department of Physics at the University of Oxford in 2019 with a mission to establish software tools capable of drastically accelerating expensive physics simulations
